name: EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689)
disclaimer: Informative only. Not a substitute for reading the published standard.

entries.title: Classification rules for high-risk AI systems
entries.summary: Establishes the criteria for classifying AI systems as high-risk based on their intended purpose and the domain of application.

entries.title: Compliance with requirements for high-risk AI systems
entries.summary: Requires providers of high-risk AI systems to ensure compliance with all requirements set out in the regulation.

entries.title: Risk management system
entries.summary: Mandates establishment of a continuous risk management system throughout the lifecycle of high-risk AI systems.

entries.title: Establishment and documentation of risk management
entries.summary: Requires a risk management system to be established, implemented, documented, and maintained as a continuous iterative process.

entries.title: Risk identification and analysis
entries.summary: Requires identification and analysis of known and reasonably foreseeable risks associated with the high-risk AI system.

entries.title: Risk estimation and evaluation
entries.summary: Mandates estimation and evaluation of risks that may emerge during intended use and reasonably foreseeable misuse.

entries.title: Risk mitigation measures
entries.summary: Requires adoption of appropriate and targeted risk management measures to address identified risks.

entries.title: Testing for risk management
entries.summary: Requires testing of the high-risk AI system to identify the most appropriate risk management measures.

entries.title: Data and data governance
entries.summary: Establishes requirements for data governance and management practices for training, validation, and testing datasets.

entries.title: Data governance practices
entries.summary: Requires data governance and management practices addressing design choices, data collection processes, and data preparation operations.

entries.title: Dataset quality criteria
entries.summary: Specifies that training, validation, and testing datasets must be relevant, sufficiently representative, and as free of errors as possible.

entries.title: Dataset characteristics consideration
entries.summary: Requires consideration of the specific characteristics, requirements, and constraints of the geographic, contextual, behavioral, or functional setting.

entries.title: Processing of special categories of data
entries.summary: Permits processing of special categories of personal data for bias monitoring and detection purposes under strict conditions.

entries.title: Technical documentation
entries.summary: Requires providers to draw up technical documentation before placing a high-risk AI system on the market.

entries.title: Record-keeping
entries.summary: Mandates that high-risk AI systems include automatic logging capabilities to ensure traceability of system functioning.

entries.title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
entries.summary: Requires high-risk AI systems to be designed to enable deployers to interpret output and use the system appropriately.

entries.title: Human oversight
entries.summary: Mandates that high-risk AI systems be designed to allow effective human oversight during the period of use.

entries.title: Human oversight measures
entries.summary: Specifies that human oversight aims to prevent or minimize risks from the high-risk AI system when used in accordance with its intended purpose.

entries.title: Accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity
entries.summary: Requires high-risk AI systems to achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity throughout their lifecycle.

entries.title: Accuracy levels
entries.summary: Requires high-risk AI systems to be designed to achieve an appropriate level of accuracy for their intended purpose, declared in instructions for use.

entries.title: Robustness and error resilience
entries.summary: Mandates that high-risk AI systems are resilient to errors, faults, and inconsistencies within the system or its environment.

entries.title: Robustness through technical redundancy
entries.summary: Specifies that robustness may be achieved through technical redundancy solutions including backup or fail-safe plans.

entries.title: Adversarial robustness
entries.summary: Requires high-risk AI systems to be resilient against attempts by unauthorized third parties to alter their use by exploiting vulnerabilities.

entries.title: Cybersecurity measures
entries.summary: Mandates appropriate cybersecurity measures to protect the AI system against unauthorized access and data manipulation.

entries.title: Technical documentation for high-risk AI systems
entries.summary: Specifies the contents of technical documentation that providers must prepare for high-risk AI systems.

entries.title: General description of the AI system
entries.summary: Requires documentation of the system's intended purpose, developer identity, version, and how the AI system interacts with hardware or software.

entries.title: Detailed description of system elements and development
entries.summary: Requires documentation of methods and steps for system development, including design specifications, architecture, and computational resources.

entries.title: Monitoring, functioning, and control description
entries.summary: Requires documentation on the system's capabilities and limitations, degree of accuracy, and foreseeable unintended outcomes.

entries.title: Risk management system description
entries.summary: Mandates documentation of the risk management system used, including identified risks and risk management measures adopted.

entries.title: Description of changes throughout lifecycle
entries.summary: Requires documentation of modifications and changes made to the system throughout its lifecycle, and their impact on compliance.

entries.title: Post-market monitoring
entries.summary: Requires providers to establish a post-market monitoring system proportionate to the nature and risks of the high-risk AI system.
